action.reasoning: Observation: The Euclidean distance heuristic with a penalty (U - 0.14*D) performed worse than the baseline Nearest Frontier (0.539 vs 0.6172). Analysis: Trading off distance for utility might cause inefficient back-and-forth travel, whereas pure Nearest Frontier naturally explores locally and minimizes travel distance. Furthermore, graph distance (hops) might be a better metric than Euclidean distance because it accounts for obstacles. Decision: I will implement pure Nearest Frontier using graph hops, with tie-breaking based on highest utility, in predict.py and submit it for evaluation. I will also run a script to compare NF Hops, NF Euclidean, and U-C*Hops on a subset of training maps.
